description_long: This is a modest, two-story brick bungalow with a gray, hip-style roof and a small covered entryway at the front. The exterior walls are constructed from reddish-brown brick, and the windows are rectangular with black or dark trim, arranged in pairs and singles on each facade. A prominent brick chimney is visible on one side, and a small wooden extension or shed is attached to the rear. The property is accompanied by two tall, coniferous trees and several urban elements such as a streetlamp, bench, and utility cover, suggesting a suburban or residential neighborhood setting. The roof is gently sloped, and the overall structure is boxy with minimal decorative detailing. The house appears solid and functional, with a simple, practical design typical of early-to-mid 20th-century suburban homes.
description: A two-story brick bungalow with a gray hip roof, rectangular windows, and a small covered entryway, accompanied by trees and urban street elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with a small porch, two front-facing windows, and adjacent trees and street furniture.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the brick chimney, paired windows, and the hip roof, with trees positioned to the right.
description_view_2: Rear and side view displaying the wooden extension, back windows, and the two tall coniferous trees in the foreground.
description_view_3: Direct rear view showing the back facade, wooden extension, and a clear arrangement of urban elements and trees in front.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with grouped and single windows on each facade
